pi_items.source: verifier.py and astronomical calculations of the March 14, 2025 total lunar eclipse

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on a synthetically generated GMAT script simulating a hypothetical LunarSat spacecraft in a 100km polar orbit during the real Total Lunar Eclipse of March 14, 2025. The script paths and output requirements exactly match the task setup and verifier logic. The expected umbra duration minimum of 3500.0 seconds is astronomically accurate, as the totality phase of the real March 2025 eclipse lasts approximately 65 minutes (~3900 seconds), guaranteeing the spacecraft will remain in continuous shadow for longer than the 3500.0 second threshold.
